entry.title: Re: Delay items part of curricula to become available to...
entry.content:
HI @YashwanthP  & @Gi ,

I agree and disagree with what you say Yash, as "Yes" it isn't possible to do this with a single Curricula & Assignment Profile.  But it is possible if you use a combination of three Curricula & associated Assignment Profiles.

Let's say that you have the end goal of Learners being competent in "Curricula ABC" (which contains the Learning Items A, B, & C) by the Assignment Profile that has the rules to include Learners that have the "Job Code" = "JC00159" and in the "Org (including sub Org's)" = "Corp (753951)", but as you say you want them to complete the Items in order without any of the later Items being seen on their Learning Plan (even if you have a staggered Required by / Initial Period).

If you first assign them "Curricula A" (which contains only the single Learning Item A) by the Assignment Profile A that has the rules to include Learners that have the "Job Code" = "JC00159" and in the "Org (including sub Org's)" = "Corp (753951)", but do not have Learning History for Item A.

Once they complete Item A, then they will not met the Assignment Profile A rules and it will remove "Curricula A", but this is when "Curricula AB" (which contains the Learning Items A & B) is assigned by the Assignment Profile AB that has the rules to include Learners that have the "Job Code" = "JC00159" and in the "Org (including sub Org's)" = "Corp (753951)", but do not have Learning History for Item B but does have learning history for Item A.

Once again when they complete Item B, then they will not met the Assignment Profile AB rules and it will remove "Curricula AB", but this is when "Curricula ABC" (which contains the Learning Items A, B & C) is assigned by the Assignment Profile ABC that has the rules to include Learners that have the "Job Code" = "JC00159" and in the "Org (including sub Org's)" = "Corp (753951)", and the Learning History for Item B and Item A.

I know that from an admin point of view that this is messy, but the end user/Learner gets the seemless experience of only ever seeing the single Item in their Learning Plan. 

I used a simplier version of this a lot with Inductions where the First/Initial training is a Instructor Led but the Refresher can be Online (which the Instructor Led is a substitute for) and it works brilliantly.

Note: In this example I often add the preceeding Item as a prerequisite because I've found that they will try and short cut their training if they can and just do the Online Refresher training

entry.title: Quality inspection at outbound with Extended warehouse management (EWM)
entry.content:
Inspection at outbound is demanded by many customers for specific products. Inspection on EWM managed outbound delivery was not possible till S/4HANA 2025 FPS0 release. Capability of inspection on outbound delivery with EWM has been introduced with S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release. My present blog is to discuss on the functionality and the prerequisite configurations to achieve inspection with EWM managed outbound delivery.

A typical process flow for inspection for EWM managed outbound delivery is depicted in below image.

An outbound delivery order (ODO) 80004946 relevant for Q-Inspection at outbound at item level has been picked , packed but not yet goods issued.

An inspection lot 100000000050 has been created in S/4HANA for the material FG1015 for the outbound delivery 80004946 from sales order 10005725.

If the goods issue is triggered on the EWM ODO, then an error message appears aborting the goods issue as the usage decision on inspection lot is pending or the inspection lot has not been accepted.

Pre-requisite configuration and master data are

  • Inspection object type 8 (Q-Inspection Outbound) needs to be activated at SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Quality Management -> Basics and Integration -> Define and Activate Warehouse-Dependent IOTs.

  • Inspection lot origin needs to be assigned to delivery type

  • Inspection type needs to be assigned to delivery category

  • Quality info record needs to be created for the material with customer and sales org.

This functionality also works for

  • Embedded and decentral EWM (with backend S/4HANA 2025 FPS01)
  • Delivery type LO Delivery w/o Ref.
  • Material with batch management

QM with EWM outbound is a simple and useful solution closing a gap for feature parity of EWM with IM.

This blog is based on my personal tests , observations , insights in S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release. Will appreciate your feedback / comments.

entry.title: Re: Production Version automatic switch when BOM componen...
entry.content: If you want true stock‑sensitive automation, the best practice is to implement the production version determination BAdI. That way, the system dynamically chooses the correct BOM alternative based on inventory, without requiring manual switching or scheduled jobs.

entry.title: Re: Master Data Material Best Practice
entry.content:
You can explore the below approaches

1) Material Master Governance
Introduce a central master data team or workflow approval for new material creation. Users request new materials, but descriptions are standardized by the governance team.

2) Description Standardization Rules
Define a naming convention (e.g., “Product Type – Brand – Model – Specification”). Train users and enforce rules via documentation or validation checks.

3) Use Classification / Characteristics
Instead of relying only on free‑text descriptions, use material classification (e.g., brand, model, type as separate fields). This makes searching and reporting easier and reduces reliance on description text.

4) Duplicate Check Tools
Implement a duplicate check during material creation. Use SAP standard duplicate check (if available in your release). Or build a custom search routine that compares new descriptions against existing ones (ignoring word order, using keywords).

5) Leverage Product Hierarchy / Catalog
If spare parts or equipment are catalogued, link materials to a product hierarchy or catalog code. This ensures consistency and reduces free‑text variation.

entry.title: Re: Review Booklet - Create a Navigation Target
entry.content:
Good day @leonor_brites 

Thank you for your questions and screenshots.

The most likely cause is that the navigation target is passing a fixed text value instead of the actual document number field from your booklet row.

In your last screenshot, the target app opens and Order is filled with SALESORDER as literal text. That means the navigation itself is working, but the parameter value the system passed is wrong. So, your target receives exactly what you maintained.

See the following reference for more details

Creating Generic Report Application | SAP Help Portal

So, if you maintain parameter name as SalesDocument and value as SalesOrder, then the target app gets the SalesOrder text, not the row’s sales order number. This is exactly consistent with your screenshots, where the target app opens but shows SALESORDER in the document field instead of the real sales order ID.

Question: What could be causing this issue?

Answer: Most likely, you configured the navigation target with a static parameter value instead of a dynamic field mapping from the booklet data provider.

Question: Is there a configuration step that I might have missed?

Answer: Usually, the missing step is not on the app you are targeting itself. The missing step is in the navigation target mapping which you must ensure that:

  • The parameter is pointing to the actual source field that contains the sales order number
  • Not to a fixed text such as SalesOrder.

See the following reference for more details

Creating Generic Report Application | SAP Help Portal

I recommend you check you setup and confirm:

  • That the Semantic Object and Action keeps the one that correctly opens the target app
  • Your Field-level mapping contains the real sales order number
  • Your Application Parameter is only used if the target app truly requires a technical inbound parameter, and then bind it to the actual row value, not a fixed label.

Here is a quick guid for your system:

In your Navigation target, open Sales order navigation target

Then check the parameter row, if the value is something like SalesOrder, remove that fixed value.

At the field level, bind the navigation to the field in the booklet data source that contains the reals sales order number, run the booklet again and click the sales order link.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/634525bea37b43d88a608e402221c932.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Automatic Clearing of the pre-paid Withholding tax GL...
entry.content:
Good day @Komalb 

Thank you for your questions and screenshots.

What you want is understandable, but based on the standard Public Cloud behaviour, there is no documented standard configuration that automatically copies the invoice document reference into the payment document’s prepaid withholding tax line so that the two G/L items can be matched for automatic clearing. So, that means:

  • Automatic clearing is only possible if the invoice-side and payment-side open items share the same clearing criteria
  • If that common criterion is not populated on both documents, the system will not clear them automatically

For clarification, Clear Open Items-Automatic Clearing and related General Ledger Jobs is the equivalent app of classic F.13 In Public Cloud. See KBA 3147360 for more insights. So, the process to test is not classical on premise F.13 directly, but the Public Cloud automatic clearing framework.

The reason why your current idea does not work is because your proposed design depends on the is logic:

  1. Invoice document prepaid WHT line gets a reference or assignment,
  2. Payment document prepaid WHT clearing line gets the same reference or
  3. Assignment and automatic clearing matches both.

That would only work if the same matching field is truly present on both items. However, from your description:

  • The invoice side contains a usable reference
  • The payment side does not populate the same reference
  • Therefore, there is a lack of a common grouping criterion for automatic clearing to take place.

It is important to note that the key requirement remains that the same value must exist on both open times. See KBA 3147360 for more insights.

The recommended approach is to:

  1. Use automatic clearing only if you can establish a true common criterion. Which means, if both invoice and payment postings carry the same Assignment, Reference, or any other grouping field, then you can use Clear Open Items-Automatic Clearing app for the prepaid WHT G/L account. This would only work if both invoic and payment lines have the same value.
  2. Clear manually is the safe standard fallback. This means, if the payment document does not carry a shared criterion, then the safer and standard fallback is to use Clear G/L Accounts-Manual Clearing for this prepaid WHT account. This is most reliable if your system cannot generate a reliable shared key.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/a84817567f98692de10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

So, the best-practice interpretation conclusion for your Mexico scenario:

  • The standard withholding tax accounting flow is fine
  • Automatic clearing is not driven by tax logic alone
  • It still requires you having a shared clearing key
  • If no standard common field is populated on both postings, then manual G/L clearing is the correct process.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris
